summary.intent: Implement the RecencyPreserving compaction mode (moltis-h0c) for the moltis chat session history compaction system, which was previously stubbed out as NotYetImplemented.
summary.outcome: RecencyPreserving mode fully implemented in compaction_run.rs with head/tail boundary preservation, tool call pruning, middle-cut marker insertion, and 10 passing tests. All clippy/fmt/test gates green; issue closed and committed.

repo: The project uses a nightly Rust toolchain pinned to nightly-2025-11-30 for fmt/clippy.
Cargo features: `llm-compaction` is default-on and gates the LlmReplace streaming-summary path; `--no-default-features` builds must stay clean.
Compaction modes are tracked via beads issues (moltis-dxw epic with child issues moltis-g37, moltis-h0c, moltis-aff, moltis-8me).
validate.rs emits semantic `not-implemented` warnings for stubbed CompactionMode variants that reference their tracking issues by name.

The default model context window is 200K tokens, which makes tail budgets enormous — tests that need to trigger middle-cut behavior must call the private strategy function directly with a small synthetic context window.

code.finding: RecencyPreserving strategy implemented here: head/tail boundary calculation, tool-call content pruning above char threshold, PRUNED_TOOL_PLACEHOLDER sentinel, middle-cut marker insertion, and dispatcher routing. TooSmallToCompact error variant added.
Semantic warning for RecencyPreserving removed now that the mode is implemented; remaining stubs (Structured) still emit not-implemented warnings.
CompactionMode::RecencyPreserving rustdoc updated from stub description to accurate trade-off documentation.
Status markers updated: recency_preserving now shown as shipped; comparison table and config reference reflect live behavior.

workflow: When tests can't trigger a code path due to environment defaults (e.g., 200K context window), call the private strategy function directly in tests with controlled parameters rather than going through the public dispatcher.
Clippy's `collapsible_if` lint requires combining nested if-guards into a single compound condition.
fmt and clippy are run as separate gates; fmt issues (closure inlining) can pass clippy and vice versa — both must be checked independently.
Issues are claimed before implementation begins and closed after the commit lands.

friction: Default 200K context window prevented dispatcher-level tests from exercising the middle-cut path — required refactoring tests to call the private strategy directly.
Multiple rounds of clippy/fmt iteration: collapsible_if and inline closure formatting required separate fixes after initial compile success.
fmt check and clippy check caught different issues, requiring several edit-check cycles before achieving a fully clean state.
open_items: moltis-aff (Structured compaction mode) — blocked on moltis-h0c merge, still returns NotYetImplemented.
moltis-8me (auxiliary-model subsystem) — p3 follow-up, no assignee, deferred until someone needs a cheap auxiliary model.
PR #653 not yet merged to main; moltis-h0c and moltis-aff are planned as two separate follow-up PRs after merge.
